## WrapperSpec.stopsChainFlags property

Exact flag tokens whose mere presence means this wrapper's own invocation \*is\* the effective command — nothing after it is ever the wrapped command, regardless of what other words follow. E.g. `command -v rm` doesn't execute `rm` at all; it prints whether `rm` is a recognized command name (Bash Reference Manual §4.1). Checked independently of word position (not "the Nth flag"), and takes precedence the moment it's seen — see `command`<!-- -->'s table entry.

**Signature:**

```typescript
readonly stopsChainFlags?: readonly string[];
```
